_comment: CANONICAL SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the train to crazy town. Every representation is generated from or reads this file: allocate.py (portfolio math) reads `orgs` + `stops` + `coeff_defaults`; diagram/train_tree.json (the draw.io graph) is generated from `stops`/`edges`/`orgs`; squiggle/base_model.squiggle (slate + soup-kitchen BOTEC blocks) and squiggle/nodes/*.squiggle (one per stop) are generated from this file. DO NOT hand-edit the generated files - edit this one and run `python3 data/generate.py`; `python3 data/test_sync.py` asserts everything stays in sync. THE MODEL IS COMPOSITIONAL: every node holds a `coeffs` record; a node's coeffs are its parent's coeffs (`coeff_defaults` at the root) with the ONE crucial-consideration delta in its `sets` merged on top. Each Squiggle node imports its PARENT node and `Dict.merge`s its `sets`, so a branch that adds one term (a discount rate, a P(nuclear war), an override) is one line different from its parent - the mimicry down the tree is real and deliberate. Numbers keep their provenance: animal/invertebrate `x_givewell` figures are from published EA Forum CEAs (see each org's `source`/`source_url`); the soup kitchen is a worked BOTEC; human/future/x-risk figures are illustrative placeholders. squiggle `daly_per_usd` are order-of-magnitude BOTECs (`[lo, hi]` = lognormal 90% CI). `top_pick` is the illustrative narrative winner shown on the diagram and can differ from allocate.py's argmax (they are deliberately different valuation lenses over the same inputs).
_coeff_comment: coeff_defaults is the ROOT coefficient record: the value every node starts from before any crucial consideration is accepted. A node's resolved coeffs = its parent's coeffs with `sets` merged on top (Dict.merge in Squiggle; dict update in Python). w_<domain> = moral weight on that domain (0 = outside the circle). neuron_exponent scales animal welfare range ~(neurons/humanNeurons)^exp. future_discount multiplies the future_human domain (1 = no pure-time discount, <1 discounts). catastrophe_mult multiplies future+xrisk value (a raised P(nuclear war)/yr lifts resilience & x-risk work). accept_tiny_prob toggles the fanatical x-risk tail. count_soil turns on the ~10^19 soil-animal reversal. override REPLACES every charity's value: 'boltzmann' sets them all to boltzmann_level (one equal pleasant thought), 'antirealist' makes them all negative (better to be selfish).

stops.label:
Parochial
community + nation
stops.desc: Least-crazy end of the line. What matters is humans in my family and local community, in the present generation. Moral weight only on people near me; heavy pure-time discount; humans only; only near-certain, measurable effects count. Distant strangers, animals and the future are outside the circle (weight 0), so the local option wins. The agreed basis for the soup kitchen is a worked BOTEC: people made happier per dollar times their wellbeing gain, netted against the counterfactual of the money sitting in a bank (which still does ~30% as much good). Every node downstream imports THIS number unchanged. The pre-EA commonsense baseline; no single EA figure defends it.

stops.label:
Humanitarian
all present humans
stops.desc: Flip: drop the nationality discount (Peter Singer's famine argument is the origin). What matters is people in the present generation, wherever they live. A life saved abroad counts like a life saved at home, so cheap, measurable global-health and cash interventions dominate. Alexander Berger is the prominent exemplar of deliberately getting off the train here and staying with robust, evidence-backed global health & wellbeing (GiveWell / Open Phil). The soup kitchen's agreed DALY is still carried through untouched; it is simply no longer the best buy.

stops.label:
Farmed
animals count
stops.desc: Flip: extend the moral circle to farmed vertebrates, weighted by a sentience-adjusted welfare range (neuron-count exponent). Peter Singer (Animal Liberation) is the canonical articulator; Lewis Bollard leads Open Phil's farm-animal-welfare work. Corporate welfare campaigns avert enormous suffering per dollar and overtake human global health under most welfare-range estimates.

stops.label:
Invertebrates
count
stops.desc: Flip: count invertebrates, with welfare range ~ (neuron count)^exponent. Brian Tomasik is the prominent voice on insect / invertebrate suffering. This is the worked example of the train's instability: sweeping the exponent from 0 to 2 swings shrimp welfare range across ~12 orders of magnitude and flips the ranking (edit neuron_exponent in this stop's `sets` to watch the top pick move). It is the FORK point: the tree splits here into a soil-animal branch (A) and a longtermist branch (F).

stops.label:
Soil animals
count (branch A)
stops.desc: Branch A (biological micro-minds): once you count the ~10^19 soil animals affected by land use, GiveWell top charities' cropland effect works out to ~1.11 kQALY/$ (~1.74x the Shrimp Welfare Project Humane Slaughter Initiative). The 'normal' human charity leaps to ~1.11e5x GiveWell's own direct value - and its SIGN is uncertain (it could be net-negative for soil nematodes). This branch has no Squiggle model yet: there is no comparable published soil-animal BOTEC to copy, so it is not fabricated.

stops.label:
Future
people count
stops.desc: Branch F (longtermist): stop discounting future lives (future_discount stays 1). Toby Ord (The Precipice) and Will MacAskill (What We Owe The Future) are the prominent longtermists; Ajeya Cotra coined the 'train to crazy town' metaphor and rides roughly to here via worldview diversification. Reducing global-catastrophic risk (e.g. ALLFED's civilisational-resilience work) starts to dominate present-focused giving. This node is itself a FORK: below it sit near-identical children that differ by exactly one added term - a pure-time discount, a raised P(nuclear war), or the fanatical x-risk tail. The fanatical tail is still refused here (accept_tiny_prob: false).

stops.label:
Future
discounted
stops.desc: Sibling of the longtermist stop, one term different: keep future people in the circle but apply a positive pure-time discount rate (future_discount 0.2). This is the cleanest illustration of the tree's mimicry - the whole calculation is identical to its parent except for a single multiplicative discount term on the future_human domain. A modest discount is enough to pull the winner back toward present, measurable global health.

stops.label:
Nuclear war
non-negligible
stops.desc: Another one-term sibling: accept a non-negligible annual probability of nuclear war. The only change from the parent is a catastrophe multiplier (catastrophe_mult 50) that raises the value of future-protecting and resilience work; every other line of the calculation is copied. Civilisational-resilience work (ALLFED) dominates because it directly buys down the tail it now takes seriously.

stops.label:
Astronomical
stakes
stops.desc: Accept Nick Bostrom's astronomical-waste argument and take small-probability / huge-payoff bets at face value (accept_tiny_prob flips true, releasing the fanatical tail); Eliezer Yudkowsky pushes AI-risk reasoning to its stark conclusions. Existential-risk reduction dominates everything. This is the far end Cotra's near-termist deliberately gets off before; ride further and the last two stops stop being about charity at all.

stops.label:
Boltzmann brain
hedonic solipsism
stops.file: Only my own present pleasure is real
stops.desc: Off the end of the line: if you are more likely a fleeting Boltzmann brain than an embodied agent, the only thing that matters is whatever makes you personally happy right now. This override INVALIDATES every assumption above it: the moral circle, welfare ranges, discounting and x-risk all stop mattering, and every charity collapses to the same low, pleasant-thought level of DALY (boltzmann_level). Nothing you do for anyone else registers, so the ranking goes flat - there is no best buy.

stops.label:
Moral anti-realism
nothing matters
stops.file: There is no moral fact; better to be selfish
stops.desc: The other terminus: if moral realism is false there is no stance-independent reason to help anyone, so from the only perspective left - your own - every dollar given away is a dollar of personal value lost. This override sets ALL DALYs negative: the whole ranking inverts and the 'best' charity is merely the least self-costly. The consistent recommendation is to keep the money; the soup kitchen shows up on top only because it moves the least.
